arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Non-SDK-style project format** — uses `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`
2. **Explicit file includes** — `<Compile Include="..."/>` for each .cs file (SDK-style auto-globs these)
3. **Debug/Release PropertyGroups with defaults** — all the Configuration/Platform conditional property groups contain default values that SDK-style projects handle automatically
4. **Explicit framework references** — `<Reference Include="System"/>`, etc. are implicit in SDK-style


5. **AssemblyInfo.cs** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes; these can move to project properties
6. **ProjectGuid** — not needed in SDK-style
7. **FileAlignment** — default, not needed
8. **AutoGenerateBindingRedirects** — default for exe projects, not needed for library
9. **Deterministic** — default true in SDK-style
10. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** — should become `TargetFramework: net472`

I'll modernize the project file and remove AssemblyInfo.cs.

</Project>

data.content:
Here's a summary of every legacy pattern removed and what replaced it:

| Legacy Pattern | What Changed |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, XML declaration | Replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` attribute |
| `Import Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit in SDK-style projects |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| `OutputType Library` | Dropped — `Library` is the SDK default |
| `ProjectGuid` | Dropped — not needed in SDK-style |
| `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Dropped — all SDK defaults |
| `Configuration`/`Platform` conditional PropertyGroups | Dropped — Debug/Release defaults are built-in |
| `<Compile Include="..." />` items | Dropped — SDK auto-globs all `*.cs` files |
| `<Reference Include="System" />` etc. | Dropped — implicit framework references |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Deleted — attributes moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) |

The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines** while preserving all behavior.
